data.description:

Approach 1: Freshworks Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model projects the cash a company is expected to generate in the future and then discounts those cash flows back to today to estimate what the business might be worth now.

For Freshworks, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach built on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month Free Cash Flow is reported at about $225.1 million. Analyst inputs extend out a few years, with Simply Wall St extrapolating further to estimate Free Cash Flow of $493.4 million by 2035, based on a path that runs through $249.5 million in 2026 and $326.9 million in 2028.

When all those projected cash flows are discounted back to today, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $23.99 per share. At the recent share price of $8.03, this implies a discount of roughly 66.5%. This indicates that the market price is well below the model’s estimate of value.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Freshworks is undervalued by 66.5%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 52 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Freshworks.

Approach 2: Freshworks Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of earnings, which is often how the market frames value in mature or consistently profitable names.

What counts as a “normal” P/E really depends on how fast earnings are expected to grow and how risky those earnings look. Higher growth or more predictable earnings can justify a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher perceived risk usually points to a lower one.

Freshworks currently trades on a P/E of 12.42x. That sits below the Software industry average P/E of 29.37x and well below the peer group average of 61.84x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Freshworks is 3.95x. This is the P/E level it estimates could be appropriate after factoring in elements like earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and key risks.

The Fair Ratio is more tailored than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for company specific characteristics rather than assuming one size fits all. Comparing 12.42x with the Fair Ratio of 3.95x, the current valuation comes out higher than that model suggests.

Result: OVERVALUED

data.title: LivaNova’s Aura6000 FDA Win Reshapes Obstructive Sleep Apnea Opportunity
data.description:
• FDA grants premarket approval to LivaNova's aura6000 System for adult patients with moderate to severe Obstructive Sleep Apnea who do not benefit from standard therapies.
  • The system uses proximal hypoglossal nerve stimulation and is supported by clinical trial data showing meaningful outcomes, including in harder to treat patient groups.
  • LivaNova (NasdaqGS:LIVN) also discloses work on a next generation, MRI compatible version of the aura6000 with additional features.

LivaNova, listed on NasdaqGS:LIVN, operates in the medical device space with a focus that includes therapies for obstructive sleep apnea. The aura6000 approval adds a new option in a segment where many patients struggle with existing treatments such as continuous positive airway pressure. Interest in sleep related health solutions has been rising as clinicians and payors pay more attention to long term impacts on cardiovascular and cognitive outcomes.

The company’s disclosure of an upcoming MRI compatible system indicates continued investment in its OSA platform and potential expansion into broader patient groups who require advanced imaging. For investors, the key questions now center on adoption by sleep specialists, reimbursement pathways, and how this product line fits alongside other device based OSA treatments.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for LivaNova by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on LivaNova.

We've flagged 0 risks for LivaNova. See which could impact your investment.

For LivaNova, FDA approval of aura6000 moves its obstructive sleep apnea work from clinical promise into a commercial product in the US, in a segment where many patients do not stay on first line therapies such as PAP. The OSPREY trial data, with reductions in apnea-hypopnea index and oxygen desaturation index and a 65% responder rate at 12 months for the treatment arm, helps position p-HGNS as a credible alternative in a market that also includes players such as Inspire Medical Systems and large diversified device makers like ResMed and Philips. The broad inclusion criteria, including patients at higher risk of complete concentric collapse, may matter for addressable patient numbers if physicians see results as representative of real world practice. Looking ahead, the disclosed work on an MRI compatible, remotely configurable next generation device with a long lasting battery suggests LivaNova is planning a platform that could support follow on revenue from replacements and programming services, subject to reimbursement and uptake. For investors, the key unknowns remain pricing, coverage decisions and how quickly sleep centers integrate aura6000 into their treatment algorithms compared with existing neurostimulation options.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The LivaNova Narrative

  • The move from clinical trial to FDA approved OSA neurostimulation fits with the narrative that clinical progress and regulatory momentum can open new therapy areas and support higher margin product mixes over time.
  • Stronger focus on OSA also raises execution risk around R&D intensity and launch costs, which could pressure margins if adoption or reimbursement do not track the expectations outlined in the broader narrative.
  • The MRI compatible, remotely managed next generation system and potential recurring revenue from long lasting implants and follow up services are not explicitly detailed in the narrative and could influence how investors think about future earnings quality.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for LivaNova to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ aura6000 will depend on favorable reimbursement decisions and hospital economics, and tighter cost controls in sleep medicine could limit procedure volumes or pricing.
  • ⚠️ Competition from existing OSA treatments, including PAP and other neurostimulation devices from companies such as Inspire Medical Systems, ResMed and Philips, may constrain share gains in key centers.
  • 🎁 FDA approval supported by a randomized controlled trial with clinically meaningful sleep metrics gives LivaNova a differentiated entry in a large pool of PAP intolerant patients.
  • 🎁 The planned MRI compatible, remotely configurable device with a long lasting rechargeable battery could support a more attractive product ecosystem and service opportunities if adopted by sleep specialists.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, focus on how quickly LivaNova secures broad insurer and Medicare coverage for aura6000, whether leading sleep centers adopt p-HGNS into treatment pathways, and how the company communicates procedure volumes and physician feedback over time. Progress on the PMA supplement for the MRI compatible next generation device, along with any commentary on pricing, battery replacement cycles and remote management, will also help you gauge how meaningful OSA could become within LivaNova’s neuromodulation portfolio.

Approach 1: Magnum Ice Cream Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company could be worth today by projecting future cash flows and then discounting them back to the present using a required return. It is essentially asking what all those future euros of cash are worth in today’s terms.

For Magnum Ice Cream, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections in €. The latest twelve month free cash flow is reported at €158.61 million. Analyst estimates and extrapolations point to projected free cash flow of €911.00 million in 2030, with annual figures stepping up across the 10 year projection period provided.

When all these projected cash flows are discounted back and summed, the DCF model produces an estimated intrinsic value of €40.95 per share. Compared with the current share price of €12.88, this implies the stock is 68.6% undervalued under these assumptions.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: Magnum Ice Cream Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a commonly used yardstick because it links what you pay per share to the earnings that each share generates. It helps you see how many euros the market is currently willing to pay for each euro of earnings.

What counts as a "normal" P/E ratio usually reflects how the market views a company’s growth prospects and risk profile. Higher expected growth or perceived resilience can justify a higher multiple, while more uncertainty or weaker prospects can lead to a lower one.

Magnum Ice Cream currently trades on a P/E of 26.91x. This sits above the Food industry average P/E of 15.25x and also above the peer average of 17.97x, which suggests investors are paying a higher price for its earnings compared with these benchmarks.

A Narrative is your story about the company, written in numbers. You set assumptions for fair value, future revenue, earnings and margins, then see what those assumptions imply for what the share could be worth.

data.title: Is GPGI (GPGI) Share Price Outrunning Fundamentals After Recent Volatility?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether GPGI is attractively priced or already reflecting a lot of optimism, the current share price at US$17.06 raises some useful questions about what you are really paying for.
  • Recent returns are mixed, with the stock up 3.3% over the last week but showing a 27.4% decline over 30 days, an 8.6% decline year to date, and gains of 51.0% over 1 year, 191.8% over 3 years, and 114.8% over 5 years.
  • These moves sit against a backdrop of ongoing coverage that keeps GPGI on investors' radars, including regular updates on its financial health, competitive position, and how the market is pricing growth expectations. This steady flow of information helps shape sentiment around both the risks and the potential embedded in the current price.
  • Yet despite the long term share price record, GPGI only scores 1 out of 6 on a simple valuation check. It is therefore worth comparing different valuation methods next, before looking at a broader way to think about what the stock is really worth.

GPGI scores just 1/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: GPGI Dividend Discount Model (DDM) Analysis

The Dividend Discount Model looks at the cash you actually receive as a shareholder by projecting future dividends and discounting them back to today. It is most useful when a company has a clear, sustainable dividend profile.

For GPGI, the model uses a dividend per share of US$0.01, a return on equity of 34.637%, and an implied payout ratio of 16.6086%. Together, these feed into an expected dividend growth rate of about 40.39% decline, based on the formula noted in the data. That negative growth input means the model is effectively assuming that dividends are not on a steady upward path.

On these inputs, the DDM output is an intrinsic value of about US$0.02 per share. Compared with the current share price of US$17.06, the model suggests the stock is very richly priced, with an implied overvaluation of around 83,456.8% according to this specific framework.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Dividend Discount Model (DDM) analysis suggests GPGI may be overvalued by 83456.8%. Discover 52 high quality undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for GPGI.

Approach 2: GPGI Price vs Book Value

For companies where book value still gives a useful anchor, the P/B ratio can help you gauge how much you are paying for each dollar of net assets. It is often used alongside other metrics for profitable businesses to see whether the market price is stretching far beyond the underlying balance sheet.

Higher growth expectations and lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/B multiple, while slower growth or higher risk usually means a lower, more cautious range is seen as fair. GPGI currently trades on a P/B of 20.32x. That sits well above the Tech industry average of 1.48x and also above the peer average of 8.77x, so the market is placing a much richer value on GPGI’s equity base than on many peers.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio is a proprietary estimate of what the P/B should be, given factors like earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and specific risks. This tends to be more tailored than simple peer or sector comparisons, which treat very different businesses as if they deserve the same multiple. There is no Fair Ratio available for GPGI here, so it is not possible to judge whether 20.32x points to overvaluation, undervaluation, or something closer to fair value using this framework.

Result: ABOUT RIGHT

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Narratives on Simply Wall St’s Community page let you connect your view of GPGI’s story to a set of revenue, earnings and margin assumptions, turn that into a fair value, compare it with the current share price to help decide whether you see GPGI as closer to a buy or a sell, and then keep that view up to date as fresh news or earnings arrive.
